ai.description:
Registers the `luna.ai.*` game AI toolkit API.

Provides factory functions and UserData wrappers for AI subsystems:
AIWorld, Agent, Blackboard, StateMachine, BehaviorTree, SteeringManager,
QLearner, UtilityAI, GOAPPlanner, InfluenceMap,
Squad, and CommandQueue.

methods.name: addAgent
methods.description: Registers a new agent named `name` in this world and returns its handle.

@param name : string
@return any

- `name` — `string`: Unique identifier for the new agent.

An `Agent` handle ready for further configuration.

audio.description:
Audio Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
details for audio api-related operations and data management.
Key types exported from this module: `LuaSource`, `LuaBus`, `LuaMidiPlayer`.
Primary functions: `register()`.

All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context
and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

functions.name: newSource
functions.description: Loads an audio file and returns a source handle.

functions.name: play
functions.description: Plays the audio source from the beginning.
functions.full_doc:
Plays the audio source from the beginning.
@param id_val : any

functions.name: stop
functions.description: Stops playback of the audio source.
functions.full_doc:
Stops playback of the audio source.
@param id_val : any

functions.name: setVolume
functions.description: Sets the playback volume (0.0 - 1.0) of the source.

functions.name: getVolume
functions.description: Returns the current volume of the audio source.

functions.name: pause
functions.description: Pauses the audio source at its current position.
functions.full_doc:
Pauses the audio source at its current position.
@param id_val : any

functions.name: resume
functions.description: Resumes playback of a paused audio source from its current position.
functions.full_doc:
Resumes playback of a paused audio source from its current position.

# Parameters
- `source` — Audio source ID to resume.
@param id_val : any
functions.params_doc:
- `source` — Audio source ID to resume.
@param id_val : any

functions.name: setPitch
functions.description: Sets the pitch (playback speed) multiplier of the source.

functions.name: getPitch
functions.description: Returns the current pitch multiplier of the source.

functions.name: isPlaying
functions.description: Returns whether the audio source is currently playing.

functions.name: isPaused
functions.description: Returns whether the audio source is currently paused.

functions.name: isStopped
functions.description: Returns whether the audio source is stopped.

functions.name: setLooping
functions.description: Enables or disables looping playback for the source.

functions.name: isLooping
functions.description: Returns whether the audio source is set to loop.

functions.name: playLooping
functions.description: Plays the audio source in a continuous loop.
functions.full_doc:
Plays the audio source in a continuous loop.
@param id_val : any

functions.name: setPan
functions.description: Sets the stereo panning (-1.0 left to 1.0 right) of the source.

functions.name: getPan

functions.name: setMasterVolume
functions.description: Sets the global master volume (0.0 - 1.0).
functions.full_doc:
Sets the global master volume (0.0 - 1.0).
@param vol : number

functions.description: Returns the current master volume scale (0.0 - 1.0) applied to all audio output.

functions.description: Creates a raw PCM audio buffer for building procedurally generated sound.

Creates a raw PCM audio buffer for building procedurally generated sound.

# Parameters
- `samples` — Total number of PCM samples to allocate.
- `sampleRate` — Sample rate in Hz (e.g. 44100).
- `bitDepth` — Bit depth per sample (8 or 16).
- `channels` — Number of channels (1 = mono, 2 = stereo).

New SoundData object ID.

- `samples` — Total number of PCM samples to allocate.

@param args : MultiValue

automation.description:
Automation Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.

Registers the `luna.simulator.*` namespace for automated input simulation
via timed step scripts. Scripts inject synthetic input events into the
engine's event queue during playback.

## Exposed Lua API

| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `luna.simulator.load(name, data)` | Load a script from a Lua step table |
| `luna.simulator.loadFromToml(name, toml_str)` | Load a script from a TOML string |
| `luna.simulator.loadFile(name, path)` | Load a script from a TOML file on disk |
| `luna.simulator.unload(name)` | Remove a loaded script by name |
| `luna.simulator.hasScript(name)` | Return `true` if a script is registered |
| `luna.simulator.getScripts()` | Return an array of all registered script names |
| `luna.simulator.start(name)` | Start playback from step zero |
| `luna.simulator.stop()` | Stop playback and reset to `Idle` |
| `luna.simulator.pause()` | Pause playback at the current position |
| `luna.simulator.resume()` | Resume from a paused position |
| `luna.simulator.update(dt)` | Advance the clock and dispatch due steps |
| `luna.simulator.isRunning()` | Return `true` if actively playing |
| `luna.simulator.isPaused()` | Return `true` if paused |
| `luna.simulator.isComplete()` | Return `true` if all steps dispatched |
| `luna.simulator.getCurrentStep()` | Return the index of the next step |
| `luna.simulator.getStepCount()` | Return the total step count |
| `luna.simulator.getCurrentScript()` | Return the active script name or `nil` |
| `luna.simulator.getElapsedTime()` | Return seconds elapsed since `start` |

## Implementation Pattern

A single `Rc<RefCell<Simulator>>` is created at registration time and
captured by all closures via Rc clone. The `SharedState` borrow is held
only long enough to push events — never across yield points.

cardgame.description:
Lua bindings for `luna.cardgame.*`.

Exposes Card, Stack, StackBuilder, StackManager, CardPool, Slot, StackHistory,
and group-analysis utilities to Lua scripts via the `luna.cardgame` namespace.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
details for cardgame api-related operations and data management.
Key types exported from this module: `LuaCard`, `LuaStack`, `LuaDeckBuilder`, `LuaZoneManager`, `LuaCardPool`.
Primary functions: `register()`.

All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context
and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

functions.name: defineType
functions.description: Defines a card type in the global registry.
functions.full_doc:
Defines a card type in the global registry.
@param name : string
@param def_tbl : table

```lua
luna.cardgame.defineType("fireball", {
  category = "spell",
  subtype  = "damage",
  rarity   = "rare",
  maxPerDeck = 3,
  stats    = { damage = 10, manaCost = 3 },
  tags     = { "fire", "instant" },
  meta     = { artist = "A. Smith" },
})
```

combat.description:
Lua bindings for the `luna.combat.*` vehicle combat API.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
details for combat api-related operations and data management.
Key types exported from this module: `LuaCollisionGroupSet`, `LuaChassis`, `LuaTurret`, `LuaWeapon`, `LuaProjectile`.
Primary functions: `register()`.

All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context
and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

functions.description: Creates a new `CollisionGroupSet`.
functions.full_doc:
Creates a new `CollisionGroupSet`.
@return any

functions.name: newChassis
functions.description: Creates a new `Chassis` with the given body ID and max HP.
functions.params_doc:
- `body_id` — `integer`: Physics body ID.
- `max_hp` — `number`: Maximum hit points.

functions.name: newTurret
functions.description: Creates a new `Turret` with the given body and joint IDs.
functions.params_doc:
- `body_id` — `integer`: Physics body ID.
- `joint_id` — `integer`: Revolute joint ID.

functions.name: newWeapon
functions.description: Creates a new fire-rate `Weapon` with the given name.
functions.params_doc: - `name` — `string`: Weapon display name.

functions.name: newProjectilePool
functions.description: Creates a new `ProjectilePool` with the given capacity.
functions.params_doc:
- `size` — `integer`: Pool capacity.
- `proj_type` — `string` optional: Projectile type (default `"ballistic"`).

functions.name: newCombatWorld

Creates a new empty `CombatWorld`.
@return any

crafting.description:
Lua bindings for `luna.crafting.*`.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
details for crafting api-related operations and data management.
Key types exported from this module: `LuaRecipe`, `LuaRecipeRegistry`, `LuaStation`, `LuaCraftSkill`, `LuaCraftQueue`.
Primary functions: `register()`.

All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context
and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

data.description:
Registers the `luna.data.*` binary data, compression, hashing, and encoding API.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
details for data api-related operations and data management.
Primary functions: `register()`.

All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context
and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

functions.name: newByteData
functions.description: Creates a new mutable byte buffer of the given size.
functions.full_doc:
Creates a new mutable byte buffer of the given size.
@param value : any

functions.name: compress
functions.description: Compresses data using the given algorithm ('deflate', 'gzip', 'lz4').

functions.name: decompress
functions.description: Decompresses data using the given algorithm.

functions.name: hash
functions.description: Returns the cryptographic hash (md5, sha1, sha256) of the input.

functions.name: encode
functions.description: Encodes Lua values into a binary string using a pack-format string.
functions.params_doc:
- `format` — Pack-format string (same syntax as string.pack).
- `...` — Values to encode.
functions.returns_doc: Binary string containing the packed bytes.

functions.name: decode
functions.description: Decodes binary data from a buffer according to a pack-format string.
functions.full_doc:
Decodes binary data from a buffer according to a pack-format string.
@param format_str : string
@param encoded : string

# Parameters
- `format` — Pack-format string (same syntax as string.pack).
- `data` — Binary string or ByteData buffer to read from.
# Returns
Decoded Lua values followed by the next unread byte offset.
functions.params_doc:
- `format` — Pack-format string (same syntax as string.pack).
- `data` — Binary string or ByteData buffer to read from.
functions.returns_doc: Decoded Lua values followed by the next unread byte offset.

functions.name: parseToml
functions.description: Parses a TOML string and returns a Lua table.
functions.full_doc:
Parses a TOML string and returns a Lua table.
@param input : string

functions.name: encodeToml
functions.description: Encodes a Lua table as a TOML string.
functions.full_doc:
Encodes a Lua table as a TOML string.
@param table : table

functions.name: pack
functions.description: Packs values into a binary byte string according to a format string.

functions.name: unpack
functions.description: Unpacks values from a binary byte string according to a format string.

functions.name: getPackedSize
functions.description: Returns the total byte count that pack() would produce for the given format and values.

functions.name: newDataView
functions.description: Creates a read-only windowed view into a byte string.

debug.description:
Registers the `luna.devtools.*` runtime diagnostics and developer tools API.

Five subsystems: structured logger, hierarchical profiler, frame statistics,
file watcher, and Lua debug bridge. All module-level functions — no
registered types.

debugbridge.description:
Registers the `luna.debugbridge.*` TCP debug server API.

Embeds a JSON-over-TCP server (127.0.0.1 only) inside the running game.
External tools (VS Code extension, MCP server) connect to inspect and
control the game at runtime. Network I/O runs on a background thread;
Lua-dependent methods are queued and dispatched via `poll()` on the main thread.

Exposes `Universe` as Lua UserData (`LuaUniverse`) with entity lifecycle,
component operations, querying, systems, tags, layers, and blueprints.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
details for entity api-related operations and data management.

event.description:
Event Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
details for event api-related operations and data management.
Primary functions: `register()`.

All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context
and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

functions.name: quit
functions.description: Pushes a quit event onto the event queue, requesting the engine to stop.
functions.full_doc:
Pushes a quit event onto the event queue, requesting the engine to stop.

# Parameters
- `exitcode` — Optional integer exit code (default 0).
@param code : integer?
functions.params_doc:
- `exitcode` — Optional integer exit code (default 0).
@param code : integer?

functions.name: push
functions.description: Pushes a custom event onto the event queue.
functions.full_doc:
Pushes a custom event onto the event queue.
@param args : MultiValue

functions.name: poll
functions.description: Polls and returns the next event from the queue, or nil if empty.
functions.full_doc:
Polls and returns the next event from the queue, or nil if empty.
@return any

functions.name: clear
functions.description: Discards all pending events in the queue.
functions.full_doc: Discards all pending events in the queue.

functions.name: newSignal
functions.description: Event.

functions.name: pump
functions.description: Syncs OS-level events into the queue. In Luna2D this is a no-op (push model).
functions.full_doc: Syncs OS-level events into the queue. In Luna2D this is a no-op (push model).

functions.name: wait
functions.description: Blocks until the next event arrives or the optional timeout elapses.
functions.full_doc:
Blocks until the next event arrives or the optional timeout elapses.

# Parameters
- `timeout` — `number?`. Timeout in seconds (optional; omit to wait indefinitely).
# Returns
Event name and arguments, or nothing on timeout.
functions.params_doc: - `timeout` — `number?`. Timeout in seconds (optional; omit to wait indefinitely).
functions.returns_doc: Event name and arguments, or nothing on timeout.

functions.name: restart
functions.description: Requests that the engine restart at the beginning of the next frame.
functions.full_doc:
Requests that the engine restart at the beginning of the next frame.

Sets `SharedState::restart_requested` to `true`. The engine loop checks this flag
after each frame and restarts if set.
# Returns
`()`.

methods.name: emit
methods.description: Emits the named event, calling all registered callbacks in order.

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Removes a subscription by handle ID.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Removes all callbacks for the named event.

methods.name: clearAll
methods.description: Removes all callbacks across all events.

methods.name: getCount
methods.description: Returns the callback count for the named event.

methods.name: getTotalCount
methods.description: Returns the total callback count across all events.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the total callback count across all events.
@return any

font.description:
Lua API bindings for the `luna.font.*` font rasterizer module.

Provides `newRasterizer`, `newTrueTypeRasterizer`, `newBMFontRasterizer`,
and `newGlyphData` factory functions matching the LÖVE2D `love.font` module
surface.  Font loading delegates to the same `Font::from_bytes` path used by
`luna.graphics.newFont` so the returned `LuaFont` userdata is fully
compatible with all existing graphics API functions.

functions.name: newRasterizer
functions.description: Loads a TTF/OTF font file and returns a font userdata object.
functions.full_doc:
Loads a TTF/OTF font file and returns a font userdata object.

This is an alias for `luna.graphics.newFont` exposed on the `luna.font`
module for LÖVE2D compatibility.

functions.name: newTrueTypeRasterizer
functions.description: Loads a TTF/OTF font with an optional hinting hint (currently ignored).
functions.full_doc:
Loads a TTF/OTF font with an optional hinting hint (currently ignored).

The `hinting` parameter is accepted for API compatibility but has no
effect — fontdue uses its own hinting strategy internally.

functions.name: newBMFontRasterizer
functions.description: Stub: BMFont rasterization is not yet supported.
functions.full_doc:
Stub: BMFont rasterization is not yet supported.

Raises a descriptive Lua error so scripts fail fast with a clear
message rather than a cryptic nil-dereference.

functions.name: newGlyphData
functions.description: Returns glyph metrics for a given Unicode code point within the font.
functions.full_doc:
Returns glyph metrics for a given Unicode code point within the font.

`font` may be a `LuaFont` userdata or a raw font ID integer.
`codepoint` is a Unicode integer (e.g. `string.byte("A")` = 65).

functions.name: captureScreenshot
functions.description: Captures the current frame as an `ImageData` and passes it to `callback`.
functions.full_doc:
Captures the current frame as an `ImageData` and passes it to `callback`.

In headless or test mode this creates a blank transparent `ImageData` sized to the
current window dimensions and calls `callback` synchronously (GPU pixel readback is
deferred to future full-GPU implementation). Setting `pending_screenshot` in
`SharedState` allows engine-side code to detect that a capture was requested.
# Parameters
- `callback` — `function`. Called with one `ImageData` argument.
# Returns
Nothing.
functions.params_doc: - `callback` — `function`. Called with one `ImageData` argument.
functions.returns_doc: Nothing.

gui.description:
Lua API bindings for the `luna.gui.*` retained-mode widget system.

Registers the `luna.gui` namespace with functions for creating widgets,
managing the widget tree, routing input events, theming, focus navigation,
and toast notifications.

## Exposed Lua API

| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `luna.gui.newButton(text?)` | Create a button widget |
| `luna.gui.newLabel(text?)` | Create a text label |
| `luna.gui.newTextInput()` | Create a text input field |
| `luna.gui.newCheckbox(text?)` | Create a checkbox |
| `luna.gui.newSlider(min?, max?)` | Create a value slider |
| `luna.gui.newProgressBar(min?, max?)` | Create a progress bar |
| `luna.gui.newComboBox()` | Create a dropdown |
| `luna.gui.newList()` | Create a selectable list |
| `luna.gui.newPanel()` | Create a container panel |
| `luna.gui.newLayout(direction?)` | Create a flexbox layout |
| `luna.gui.newScrollPanel()` | Create a scrollable panel |
| `luna.gui.newNinePatch()` | Create a 9-patch slicer |
| `luna.gui.newTabBar()` | Create a tab bar |
| `luna.gui.newSeparator(vertical?)` | Create a separator line |
| `luna.gui.newSpacer(w?, h?)` | Create spacing filler |
| `luna.gui.newToast(msg?, duration?)` | Create a toast notification |
| `luna.gui.newTreeView()` | Create a collapsible tree |
| `luna.gui.newRadioButton(text?, group?)` | Create a grouped radio button |
| `luna.gui.newScrollBar(vertical?)` | Create a scroll bar |
| `luna.gui.newWindow(title?)` | Create a draggable window |
| `luna.gui.newSplitPanel(orientation?)` | Create a split panel |
| `luna.gui.newDockPanel()` | Create a dock panel |
| `luna.gui.newToolbar(orientation?)` | Create a toolbar |
| `luna.gui.newMenuBar()` | Create a menu bar |
| `luna.gui.newMenuItem(text?)` | Create a menu item |
| `luna.gui.newDialog(title?)` | Create a modal dialog |
| `luna.gui.newStatusBar()` | Create a status bar |
| `luna.gui.newAccordion()` | Create a collapsible accordion |
| `luna.gui.newTooltipPanel(text?)` | Create a tooltip panel |
| `luna.gui.newColorPicker()` | Create a color picker |
| `luna.gui.newTable()` | Create a data table |
| `luna.gui.newImageWidget()` | Create an image widget |
| `luna.gui.newTheme()` | Create a theme instance |
| `luna.gui.setTheme(theme)` | Set the active theme |
| `luna.gui.getTheme()` | Get the active theme |
| `luna.gui.getRoot()` | Get the root panel index |
| `luna.gui.setFocus(idx/nil)` | Set keyboard focus |
| `luna.gui.getFocus()` | Get focused widget index |
| `luna.gui.focusNext()` | Tab to next focusable |
| `luna.gui.focusPrev()` | Tab to previous focusable |
| `luna.gui.clearFocus()` | Clear focus |
| `luna.gui.addToast(toast)` | Queue a toast overlay |
| `luna.gui.getToastCount()` | Active toast count |
| `luna.gui.mousepressed(x,y,btn?)` | Forward mouse press |
| `luna.gui.mousereleased(x,y,btn?)` | Forward mouse release |
| `luna.gui.mousemoved(x,y)` | Forward mouse move |
| `luna.gui.keypressed(key)` | Forward key press |
| `luna.gui.textinput(text)` | Forward text input |
| `luna.gui.wheelmoved(x,y)` | Forward mouse wheel |
| `luna.gui.update(dt)` | Advance toast timers |
| `luna.gui.getWidgetCount()` | Total widget count |
| Widget methods | See per-type method tables in gui.md |

## Implementation Pattern

A single `Rc<RefCell<GuiContext>>` is created at registration time and
captured by all closures via Rc clone.  Widget indices (pool positions)
are returned to Lua as integers.  Container/list/tab indices are 1-based
in Lua.

image.description:
Registers the `luna.image.*` pixel-level image manipulation API.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
details for image api-related operations and data management.
Primary functions: `register()`.

All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context
and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

log.description:
Structured game-level logging API (`luna.log.*`).

Exposes convenience logging functions so Lua scripts can emit messages at
specific severity levels and query or change the active log level at
runtime without going through the `luna.system` namespace.

functions.name: debug
functions.description: Emit a debug-severity log message from Lua.
functions.full_doc:
Emit a debug-severity log message from Lua.
@param message : string

# Parameters
- `message` — The message string to log.
functions.params_doc: - `message` — The message string to log.

functions.description: Emit an info-severity log message from Lua.
functions.full_doc:

@param message : string

# Parameters
- `message` — The message string to log.
functions.params_doc: - `message` — The message string to log.

functions.name: print
functions.description: Emit a log message from Lua at the specified severity level.
functions.full_doc:
Emit a log message from Lua at the specified severity level.
@param level : string
@param message : string

Accepts the same level strings as `luna.log.setLevel`:
`"debug"`, `"info"`, `"warn"`, `"error"`, `"trace"`.
Unknown levels fall back to `info`.
# Parameters
- `level` — Severity level string.
- `message` — The message string to log.
functions.params_doc:
- `level` — Severity level string.
- `message` — The message string to log.

functions.name: setLevel
functions.description: Set the minimum severity level for runtime log messages.
functions.full_doc:
Set the minimum severity level for runtime log messages.
@param level : string

# Parameters
- `level` — One of `"off"`, `"error"`, `"warn"`, `"info"`, `"debug"`, `"trace"`.
functions.params_doc: - `level` — One of `"off"`, `"error"`, `"warn"`, `"info"`, `"debug"`, `"trace"`.

overlay.description:
Lua API bindings for the `luna.overlay.*` screen-effect overlay module.

Registers the `luna.overlay` table and exposes a single factory:

- `luna.overlay.newOverlay(width?, height?)` — creates a `LuaOverlay`
  UserData object.

All overlay state lives entirely inside an `Rc<RefCell<Overlay>>`; the
`LuaOverlay` wrapper holds a clone of that smart pointer. Calling any
method borrows the `RefCell` for the duration of the call, then
releases it — the engine never holds a long-lived `borrow_mut` across
Lua callbacks.

The `register` function is called once during engine startup by the
`lua_api` module registration chain; it adds the `luna.overlay` table
to the root `luna` global.

methods.name: update
methods.description: Advances all active overlay subsystems by `dt` seconds.
methods.full_doc:
Advances all active overlay subsystems by `dt` seconds.

Drives weather particle spawning and culling, flash and shake
decay, fade interpolation, cloud scroll accumulation, and
lightning deactivation. Call exactly once per frame in
`luna.update(dt)` before reading any derived state such as
`getShakeOffset` or `isFlashing`.
@param dt : number

methods.name: draw
methods.description: Placeholder for draw — actual rendering handled by the game loop.
methods.full_doc: Placeholder for draw — actual rendering handled by the game loop.

methods.name: resize
methods.description: Updates the internal canvas dimensions on window resize.

methods.name: getWidth
methods.description: Returns the overlay canvas width in pixels.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the overlay canvas width in pixels.

Reflects the value passed to `newOverlay` or the most recent
`resize` call.
@return number

methods.name: getHeight
methods.description: Returns the overlay canvas height in pixels.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the overlay canvas height in pixels.

Reflects the value passed to `newOverlay` or the most recent
`resize` call.
@return number

methods.name: getDimensions
methods.description: Returns both canvas dimensions as `(width, height)`.
methods.full_doc:
Returns both canvas dimensions as `(width, height)`.

Convenience function; equivalent to calling `getWidth()` and
`getHeight()` separately but in a single Lua call.
@return number, number

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Resets all overlay subsystems to their inactive defaults.
methods.full_doc:
Resets all overlay subsystems to their inactive defaults.

Equivalent to calling `Overlay::clear()` — drops all live weather
particles, deactivates flash/shake/fade/lightning, and disables
ambient, clouds, fog, heat haze, vignette, and film grain.
All parameters (intensity, colour, duration) are reset to
their initial values. Useful when transitioning between scenes.

methods.name: isActive
methods.description: Returns `true` if any overlay subsystem is currently active.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if any overlay subsystem is currently active.

Checks weather enabled, ambient enabled, flash/shake/fade/lightning
active, clouds enabled, fog enabled, heat haze enabled, vignette
enabled, and film grain enabled. Returns `false` when the overlay
has been cleared or was never activated.
@return boolean

methods.name: getAmbientColor
methods.description: Returns the current ambient tint colour as `(r, g, b, a)`.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the current ambient tint colour as `(r, g, b, a)`.

When time-of-day cycling is active the returned value may change
every frame as `compute_color_from_time` advances.
@return number, number, number, number

methods.name: setTimeOfDay
methods.description: Sets the time-of-day value that drives ambient colour cycling.
methods.full_doc:
Sets the time-of-day value that drives ambient colour cycling.

The value is in 24-hour floating-point format (0.0 = midnight,
12.0 = noon, 24.0 wraps to midnight again). Has no visible
effect unless ambient cycling is enabled via `setAmbientEnabled`.
@param hour : number

methods.name: getTimeOfDay
methods.full_doc:
Returns the current time-of-day value (0.0–24.0).
@return number

methods.name: setAmbientEnabled
methods.description: Enables or disables automatic time-of-day ambient colour cycling.
methods.full_doc:
Enables or disables automatic time-of-day ambient colour cycling.

When `true`, `update()` calls `compute_color_from_time()` each
frame and overwrites `ambient.color`. When `false`, the colour
set via `setAmbientColor` is used as a static tint.
@param enabled : boolean

methods.name: isAmbientEnabled
methods.description: Returns `true` if automatic ambient colour cycling is enabled.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if automatic ambient colour cycling is enabled.
@return boolean

methods.name: setWeather
methods.description: Sets the active weather type by name.
methods.full_doc:
Sets the active weather type by name.

Valid names: `"none"`, `"rain"`, `"snow"`, `"hail"`, `"dust"`,
`"leaves"`, `"ash"`, `"pollen"`. The new type takes effect on the
next `update` call — existing particles are not cleared, so there
may be a brief mix of old and new particle behaviors. Returns a
Lua error if `name` is unrecognised.
@param weather_type : string

methods.name: getWeather
methods.description: Returns the current weather type as a lowercase string name.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the current weather type as a lowercase string name.
@return string

methods.name: setWeatherIntensity
methods.full_doc:
Sets the weather particle density.

Controls both the maximum particle count (up to 200) and the
spawn rate. 0.0 stops spawning; 1.0 produces the maximum
particle density for the current weather type.
@param intensity : number

methods.name: getWeatherIntensity
methods.full_doc:
Returns the weather particle density (0.0–1.0).
@return number

methods.name: setWindDirection
methods.description: Sets the wind direction as an angle in radians.
methods.full_doc:
Sets the wind direction as an angle in radians.

0.0 blows particles to the right; `math.pi / 2` blows downward.
Combined with `setWindSpeed`, this drifts all live particles
each frame by the wind velocity vector.
@param angle : number

methods.name: getWindDirection
methods.description: Returns the wind direction in radians.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the wind direction in radians.
@return number

methods.name: setWindSpeed
methods.description: Sets the wind speed in pixels per second.
methods.full_doc:
Sets the wind speed in pixels per second.

Adds a global velocity offset to every live weather particle each
frame. 0.0 disables wind drift.
@param speed : number

methods.name: getWindSpeed
methods.description: Returns the wind speed in pixels per second.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the wind speed in pixels per second.
@return number

methods.name: setWeatherEnabled
methods.description: Enables or disables the weather particle spawner.
methods.full_doc:
Enables or disables the weather particle spawner.

When disabled, no new particles are spawned but existing ones
continue to move and age until they leave the screen. Set the
weather type to `"none"` first if you want an immediate cutoff.
@param enabled : boolean

methods.name: isWeatherEnabled
methods.description: Returns `true` if the weather particle spawner is enabled.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if the weather particle spawner is enabled.
@return boolean

methods.name: getShakeOffset
methods.description: Returns the current camera-shake pixel offset as `(x, y)`.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the current camera-shake pixel offset as `(x, y)`.

Returns `(0, 0)` when no shake is active. The renderer should
offset all draw operations by this pair to apply the shake.
@return number, number

methods.name: isFlashing
methods.description: Returns `true` while a flash is fading out.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` while a flash is fading out.
@return boolean

methods.name: isShaking
methods.description: Returns `true` while a camera shake is in progress.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` while a camera shake is in progress.
@return boolean

methods.name: isFading
methods.description: Returns `true` while a fade transition is in progress.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` while a fade transition is in progress.
@return boolean

methods.name: setCloudShadows
methods.description: Enables or disables the scrolling cloud shadow overlay.
methods.full_doc:
Enables or disables the scrolling cloud shadow overlay.

Cloud shadows are rendered as soft blob-shaped shadow blobs
drifting horizontally across the scene. Configure their
appearance with `setCloudCount`, `setCloudScale`, and
`setCloudOpacity`.
@param enabled : boolean

methods.name: isCloudShadowsEnabled
methods.description: Returns `true` if the cloud shadow overlay is enabled.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if the cloud shadow overlay is enabled.
@return boolean

methods.name: setCloudCount
methods.description: Sets the number of cloud shadow blobs rendered each frame.
methods.full_doc:
Sets the number of cloud shadow blobs rendered each frame.

Higher counts produce denser, more uniform coverage. Each blob
is positioned pseudo-randomly based on its index, so the layout
is consistent across frames.
@param count : number

methods.name: getCloudCount
methods.description: Returns the current cloud shadow blob count.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the current cloud shadow blob count.
@return number

methods.name: setCloudSpeed
methods.description: Sets the horizontal scroll speed of cloud shadows in pixels per second.
methods.full_doc:
Sets the horizontal scroll speed of cloud shadows in pixels per second.

The internal `offset` accumulator grows without bound; the renderer
wraps it modulo screen width so shadows loop seamlessly.
@param speed : number

methods.name: getCloudSpeed
methods.description: Returns the cloud shadow scroll speed in pixels per second.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the cloud shadow scroll speed in pixels per second.
@return number

methods.name: setCloudScale
methods.description: Sets the relative size scale of each cloud shadow blob.
methods.full_doc:
Sets the relative size scale of each cloud shadow blob.

1.0 is the default blob size; smaller values produce compact
dappled shadows; larger values produce wide overcast patches.
@param scale : number

methods.name: getCloudScale
methods.description: Returns the cloud shadow blob size scale.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the cloud shadow blob size scale.
@return number

methods.name: setCloudOpacity
methods.description: Sets the cloud shadow overlay opacity (0.0 = invisible, 1.0 = fully dark).
methods.full_doc:
Sets the cloud shadow overlay opacity (0.0 = invisible, 1.0 = fully dark).

Controls how darkly the shadow blobs are blended over the scene.
0.3 gives a subtle day-time look; 0.7–0.9 simulates heavy overcast.
@param opacity : number

methods.name: getCloudOpacity
methods.full_doc:
Returns the cloud shadow opacity (0.0–1.0).
@return number

methods.name: setFogEnabled
methods.full_doc:
Enables or disables atmospheric fog.
@param enabled : boolean
Enables or disables the atmospheric fog overlay.

When enabled, a translucent colour rectangle is blended over the
full scene every frame. Configure thickness via `setFogDensity`
and colour via `setFogColor`.

methods.name: isFogEnabled
methods.description: Returns `true` if the atmospheric fog overlay is enabled.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if the atmospheric fog overlay is enabled.
@return boolean

methods.name: setFogDensity
methods.description: Sets the fog density (0.0 = invisible, 1.0 = fully opaque).

methods.name: getFogDensity
methods.full_doc:
Returns the current fog density (0.0–1.0).
@return number

methods.name: getFogColor
methods.description: Returns the current fog colour as `(r, g, b, a)`.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the current fog colour as `(r, g, b, a)`.
@return number, number, number, number

methods.name: setHeatHazeEnabled
methods.description: Enables or disables UV-distortion heat shimmer.
methods.full_doc:
Enables or disables UV-distortion heat shimmer.

When enabled, the renderer applies a time-animated sine wave
displacement to the scene's UV coordinates, simulating the
wavering distortion seen above hot surfaces.
@param enabled : boolean

methods.name: isHeatHazeEnabled
methods.description: Returns `true` if heat haze distortion is enabled.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if heat haze distortion is enabled.
@return boolean

methods.name: setHeatHazeIntensity
methods.description: Sets the heat haze distortion intensity.
methods.full_doc:
Sets the heat haze distortion intensity.

Controls the peak UV displacement in pixels. 0.2–2.0 gives
subtle mirage shimmer; higher values are suitable for extreme
heat or magical warp effects.
@param intensity : number

methods.name: getHeatHazeIntensity
methods.description: Returns the heat haze distortion intensity.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the heat haze distortion intensity.
@return number

methods.name: setVignetteEnabled
methods.full_doc:
Enables or disables screen-edge darkening (vignette).

The vignette grades from transparent at the screen centre to
near-black at the corners. Useful for suggesting restricted
vision, dramatic tension, or a cinematic look.
@param enabled : boolean

methods.name: isVignetteEnabled
methods.description: Returns `true` if the vignette overlay is enabled.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if the vignette overlay is enabled.
@return boolean

methods.name: setVignetteStrength
methods.full_doc:
Sets the vignette darkening strength (0.0–1.0).

0.0 is invisible; 0.5 is a gentle filmic border; 1.0 crushes
corners to near-black.
@param strength : number

methods.name: getVignetteStrength
methods.description: Returns the current vignette darkening strength (0.0–1.0).
methods.full_doc:
Returns the current vignette darkening strength (0.0–1.0).
@return number

methods.name: setFilmGrainEnabled
methods.description: Enables or disables per-frame random film grain noise.
methods.full_doc:
Enables or disables per-frame random film grain noise.

The noise pattern is regenerated every frame so it does not
repeat or create a visible flicker texture.
@param enabled : boolean

methods.name: isFilmGrainEnabled
methods.description: Returns `true` if film grain noise is enabled.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if film grain noise is enabled.
@return boolean

methods.name: setFilmGrainIntensity
methods.description: Sets the film grain noise amplitude (0.0–1.0).
methods.full_doc:
Sets the film grain noise amplitude (0.0–1.0).

0.1–0.3 gives a subtle cinematic look. Values above 0.5
produce heavy grain that can obscure fine detail.
@param intensity : number

methods.name: getFilmGrainIntensity
methods.description: Returns the current film grain noise amplitude (0.0–1.0).
methods.full_doc:
Returns the current film grain noise amplitude (0.0–1.0).
@return number

methods.name: triggerLightning
methods.full_doc:
Triggers a one-shot lightning flash.

Fires a brief hard white flash (default 0.15 s). Calling while
a flash is in progress restarts it from full brightness.
Customise the flash colour first with `setLightningColor`.

methods.name: getLightningColor
methods.description: Returns the current lightning flash colour as `(r, g, b, a)`.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the current lightning flash colour as `(r, g, b, a)`.
@return number, number, number, number

pathfinding.description:
Registers the `luna.pathfinding.*` grid-based pathfinding API.

Exposes `NavGrid`, `UnitPathfinder`, and `FlowField` as Lua UserData
with 1-based tile coordinates at the Lua boundary.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
details for pathfinding api-related operations and data management.
Primary functions: `register()`.

All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context
and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

patterns.description:
Registers the `luna.patterns.*` software design patterns API.

Provides factory functions and UserData wrappers for reusable patterns:
EventBus (prioritized pub-sub), ObjectPool (object reuse), CommandStack
(undo/redo), ServiceLocator (DI container), Factory (named constructors),
and SimpleState (finite state machine).

functions.name: newPolygonBody
functions.description: Creates a convex polygon body defined by a list of local-space vertices.
functions.full_doc:
Creates a convex polygon body defined by a list of local-space vertices.

# Parameters
- `world` ÔÇö World ID.
- `type` ÔÇö Body type: 'static', 'dynamic', or 'kinematic'.
- `x` ÔÇö World X position for the body's origin.
- `y` ÔÇö World Y position for the body's origin.
- `vertices` ÔÇö Flat or nested table of (x, y) polygon vertices in local space.
# Returns
New body ID.
functions.params_doc:
- `world` ÔÇö World ID.
- `type` ÔÇö Body type: 'static', 'dynamic', or 'kinematic'.
- `x` ÔÇö World X position for the body's origin.
- `y` ÔÇö World Y position for the body's origin.
- `vertices` ÔÇö Flat or nested table of (x, y) polygon vertices in local space.
functions.returns_doc: New body ID.

functions.name: newEdgeBody
functions.description: Creates a single-segment edge body connecting two points in world space.
functions.full_doc:
Creates a single-segment edge body connecting two points in world space.

# Parameters
- `world` ÔÇö World ID.
- `x1`, `y1` ÔÇö Start point in world units.
- `x2`, `y2` ÔÇö End point in world units.
# Returns
New body ID.
functions.params_doc:
- `world` ÔÇö World ID.
- `x1`, `y1` ÔÇö Start point in world units.
- `x2`, `y2` ÔÇö End point in world units.
functions.returns_doc: New body ID.

functions.name: newChainBody
functions.description: Creates a static chain body from a list of connected edge segments.
functions.full_doc:
Creates a static chain body from a list of connected edge segments.

# Parameters
- `world` ÔÇö World ID.
- `vertices` ÔÇö Flat or nested table of (x, y) chain vertices.
- `closed` ÔÇö Optional boolean; true to close the chain into a loop.
# Returns
New body ID.
functions.params_doc:
- `world` ÔÇö World ID.
- `vertices` ÔÇö Flat or nested table of (x, y) chain vertices.
- `closed` ÔÇö Optional boolean; true to close the chain into a loop.
functions.returns_doc: New body ID.

functions.name: addWheelJoint
functions.description: Adds a wheel joint combining a revolute motor and a prismatic spring for vehicle suspension.
functions.full_doc:
Adds a wheel joint combining a revolute motor and a prismatic spring for vehicle suspension.

# Parameters
- `world` ÔÇö World ID.
- `body1` ÔÇö Chassis body ID.
- `body2` ÔÇö Wheel body ID.
- `ax`, `ay` ÔÇö World-space anchor point.
- `axisX`, `axisY` ÔÇö Suspension axis direction.
# Returns
New joint ID.
functions.params_doc:
- `world` ÔÇö World ID.
- `body1` ÔÇö Chassis body ID.
- `body2` ÔÇö Wheel body ID.
- `ax`, `ay` ÔÇö World-space anchor point.
- `axisX`, `axisY` ÔÇö Suspension axis direction.
functions.returns_doc: New joint ID.

functions.name: addFrictionJoint
functions.description: Adds a friction joint that resists relative movement and rotation between two bodies.
functions.full_doc:
Adds a friction joint that resists relative movement and rotation between two bodies.

# Parameters
- `world` ÔÇö World ID.
- `body1` ÔÇö First body ID.
- `body2` ÔÇö Second body ID.
- `ax`, `ay` ÔÇö World-space anchor point.
# Returns
New joint ID.
functions.params_doc:
- `world` ÔÇö World ID.
- `body1` ÔÇö First body ID.
- `body2` ÔÇö Second body ID.
- `ax`, `ay` ÔÇö World-space anchor point.
functions.returns_doc: New joint ID.

functions.name: addMotorJoint
functions.description: Adds a motor joint that drives one body toward a target position relative to another.
functions.full_doc:
Adds a motor joint that drives one body toward a target position relative to another.

# Parameters

postfx.description:
Lua API bindings for the `luna.postfx.*` post-processing effects module.

Registers the `luna.postfx` table and exposes three factory functions:

- `luna.postfx.newEffect(name)` — creates a built-in `PostFxEffect`
  by name (`"bloom"`, `"blur"`, `"crt"`, `"godrays"`, `"vignette"`,
  `"colourgrade"`, `"chromatic"`).
- `luna.postfx.newPass(shaderId)` — creates a `Custom` `PostFxEffect`
  backed by an external shader resource ID.
- `luna.postfx.newStack(width?, height?)` — creates an empty
  `PostFxStack` with the given canvas dimensions.

`LuaPostFxEffect` and `LuaPostFxStack` are thin `mlua` UserData wrappers
that hold `Rc<RefCell<...>>` smart pointers. `LuaPostFxStack` additionally
owns the concrete effect vector so that effect objects added via Lua are
kept alive as long as the stack is alive.

The `register` function is called once during engine startup.

functions.name: newEffect
functions.description: Creates a built-in post-processing effect by name.
functions.full_doc:
Creates a built-in post-processing effect by name.

Valid names: `"bloom"`, `"blur"`, `"crt"`, `"godrays"`,
`"vignette"`, `"colourgrade"`, `"chromatic"`. Returns an error
if the name is not recognised. Default parameters are populated
automatically; call `setParameter` to override them.
@param name : string
@return PostFxEffect

functions.name: newPass
functions.description: Creates a custom shader pass effect.
functions.full_doc:
Creates a custom shader pass effect.

The returned `PostFxEffect` has `effect_type = Custom` and starts
with an empty parameter map. The `shader_id` is passed to the GPU
layer during `apply()` so it can look up and dispatch the correct
user-supplied shader.
@param shader_id : integer
@return PostFxEffect

functions.name: newStack
functions.description: Creates an empty post-processing stack.

functions.name: getEffectTypes
functions.description: Returns a Lua table listing all valid built-in effect type names.
functions.full_doc:
Returns a Lua table listing all valid built-in effect type names.

Useful for introspection tools or mod loaders that enumerate
available effects without hard-coding the list.
@return table<string>

methods.name: setParameter
methods.description: Sets a named float parameter on the effect.

methods.name: hasParameter
methods.description: Returns `true` if the named parameter exists on this effect.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if the named parameter exists on this effect.

Useful for introspection and serialisation. Custom effects start
with an empty parameter map, so this will return `false` for any
name until `setParameter` is called.
# Parameters
- `name` — `string` — Parameter key to test.
# Returns
`boolean` — `true` if the key is present in the params map.
methods.returns_doc: `boolean` — `true` if the key is present in the params map.

methods.name: getParameterNames
methods.description: Returns a sorted list of all parameter names currently set on this effect.
methods.full_doc:
Returns a sorted list of all parameter names currently set on this effect.

The returned Lua table is 1-indexed and sorted alphabetically.
Useful for iterating all parameters during serialisation or
building a parameter UI.
# Returns
`table<string>` — Sorted array of parameter name strings.
methods.returns_doc: `table<string>` — Sorted array of parameter name strings.

methods.name: getEffectType
methods.description: Returns the string name of this effect's type.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the string name of this effect's type.

Corresponds to `PostFxEffectType::name()`. Returns `"custom"` for
custom shader pass effects created with `newPass`.
# Returns
`string` — One of `"bloom"`, `"blur"`, `"crt"`, `"godrays"`,
`"vignette"`, `"colourgrade"`, `"chromatic"`, or `"custom"`.

methods.name: isBuiltIn
methods.description: Returns `true` if this is a built-in effect (not a custom shader pass).
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if this is a built-in effect (not a custom shader pass).

Returns `false` for effects created with `luna.postfx.newPass`.
# Returns
`boolean`.

methods.name: isEnabled
methods.description: Returns `true` if this effect is currently enabled.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if this effect is currently enabled.

Disabled effects are skipped by the stack during `apply()` without
being removed from the chain. Enable/disable state can be toggled
at any time without affecting the effect's parameters.
# Returns
`boolean`.

methods.name: setEnabled
methods.description: Enables or disables this effect within its parent stack.
methods.full_doc:
Enables or disables this effect within its parent stack.

When disabled the effect remains in the chain at its current
position but is skipped during `apply()`. Re-enabling it restores
the pass at the same position.
# Parameters
- `enabled` — `boolean` — `true` to activate, `false` to skip.

methods.name: setThreshold
methods.full_doc:
Sets the bloom bright-pass threshold.

Pixels with luminance above this threshold contribute to the bloom
effect. Typical values are 0.5–0.9; lower values bleed bloom onto
more of the scene.
# Parameters
- `value` — `number` — Threshold luminance (0.0–1.0).

methods.name: setIntensity
methods.description: Sets the intensity for bloom or godrays effects.
methods.full_doc:
Sets the intensity for bloom or godrays effects.

For bloom: scales the brightness of the blurred highlight layer
before it is composited back onto the scene. For godrays: controls
the overall brightness of the light ray overlay.
# Parameters
- `value` — `number` — Intensity multiplier (typically 0.0–2.0).

methods.name: setScanlineStrength
methods.full_doc:
Sets the CRT scanline strength.

Controls how prominent the dark horizontal scanlines are in the
CRT monitor simulation. 0.0 disables the scanline overlay;
1.0 produces fully black lines every other row.
# Parameters
- `value` — `number` — Scanline opacity (0.0–1.0).

methods.name: setRadius
methods.full_doc:
Sets the Gaussian blur radius.

Larger values spread the blur sample footprint across more pixels.
Values above ~8 may be expensive on integrated GPUs.
# Parameters
- `value` — `number` — Blur radius in pixels.

methods.name: setStrength
methods.description: Sets the vignette or blur strength.
methods.full_doc:
Sets the vignette or blur strength.

For vignette: 0.0 is invisible; 1.0 darkens the corners to black.
For blur: scales the overall blur weight applied per sample.
# Parameters
- `value` — `number` — Strength factor (0.0–1.0).

methods.name: setOffset
methods.description: Sets the chromatic aberration pixel offset.
methods.full_doc:
Sets the chromatic aberration pixel offset.

The R, G, and B channels are sampled at offsets proportional to
this value. Larger values produce a more extreme colour-fringing
effect. Typical range is 1–5 pixels.
# Parameters
- `value` — `number` — Pixel offset for channel separation.

methods.name: setBrightness
methods.description: Sets the colour grading brightness multiplier.
methods.full_doc:
Sets the colour grading brightness multiplier.

Applied as a uniform scale across all channels before contrast
and saturation adjustments. 1.0 is neutral; values below 1.0
darken the scene; values above 1.0 brighten it.
# Parameters
- `value` — `number` — Brightness multiplier (default 1.0).

methods.name: setContrast
methods.full_doc:
Sets the colour grading contrast.

Pivots around mid-grey (0.5). Values above 1.0 push darks darker
and lights lighter; values below 1.0 flatten the tonal range.
# Parameters
- `value` — `number` — Contrast multiplier (default 1.0).

methods.name: setSaturation
methods.full_doc:
Sets the colour grading saturation.

0.0 produces a greyscale image; 1.0 is neutral; values above 1.0
boost colour vividness. Applied after brightness and contrast.
# Parameters
- `value` — `number` — Saturation multiplier (0.0–2.0, default 1.0).

methods.name: add
methods.description: Appends a `PostFxEffect` to the end of the stack.
methods.full_doc:
Appends a `PostFxEffect` to the end of the stack.

Effects are applied in insertion order during `apply()`. The effect
is registered in the local effects registry and its index is
appended to the `PostFxStack` chain. The effect is enabled by default.
# Parameters
- `effect` — `PostFxEffect` userdata — The effect to append.

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Removes a `PostFxEffect` from the stack chain.
methods.full_doc:
Removes a `PostFxEffect` from the stack chain.

Identifies the effect by pointer equality against the local registry.
After removal all subsequent effects shift down by one position.
Returns `false` if the effect is not in this stack's registry.
# Parameters
- `effect` — `PostFxEffect` userdata — The effect to remove.
# Returns
`boolean` — `true` if the effect was found and removed.
methods.returns_doc: `boolean` — `true` if the effect was found and removed.

methods.name: isEffectEnabled
methods.description: Returns `true` if the given effect is enabled within this stack.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if the given effect is enabled within this stack.

Queries the per-position enabled flag in the chain. Returns `false`
if the effect is not in this stack's registry at all. This is
distinct from `effect:isEnabled()`, which reads the enabled flag
stored directly on the `PostFxEffect` object; both flags must be
`true` for the pass to execute during `apply()`.
# Parameters
- `effect` — `PostFxEffect` userdata — The effect to query.
# Returns
`boolean` — `false` if the effect is absent from the chain.
methods.returns_doc: `boolean` — `false` if the effect is absent from the chain.

methods.name: getEffectCount
methods.description: Returns the number of effects currently in the chain (enabled and disabled).
methods.full_doc:
Returns the number of effects currently in the chain (enabled and disabled).

Useful for iterating via `getEffect(i)` from Lua.
# Returns
`integer` — Total effects in the chain.

methods.name: getEffect
methods.description: Returns the effect at a 1-based position in the chain, or `nil`.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the effect at a 1-based position in the chain, or `nil`.

Provides random access into the chain. Returns the
`LuaPostFxEffect` UserData at that position, or `nil` if `index`
is out of range. The returned object is shared — mutations to it
are visible immediately through any other Lua variable holding the
same effect.
# Parameters
- `index` — `integer` — 1-based position in the chain.
# Returns
`PostFxEffect | nil`.

methods.name: getEnabledEffects
methods.description: Returns a Lua table of all currently enabled effects in application order.
methods.full_doc:
Returns a Lua table of all currently enabled effects in application order.

Used by the GPU layer during `apply()` to determine which shader
passes to run. Disabled effects are excluded. The returned table
is 1-indexed and contains `PostFxEffect` UserData objects.
# Returns
`table<PostFxEffect>` — Enabled effects in chain order.

methods.name: resize
methods.full_doc:
Resizes the internal canvas dimensions.

Call this whenever the window or render target changes size so the
GPU layer can recreate ping-pong canvases at the correct resolution.
Does not remove or invalidate any effects in the chain.
# Parameters
- `w` — `integer` — New canvas width in pixels.
- `h` — `integer` — New canvas height in pixels.
methods.params_doc:
- `w` — `integer` — New canvas width in pixels.
- `h` — `integer` — New canvas height in pixels.

methods.name: getWidth
methods.description: Returns the canvas width in pixels.
methods.full_doc:

`integer`.

methods.name: getHeight
methods.description: Returns the canvas height in pixels.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the canvas height in pixels.

`integer`.

methods.name: getDimensions
methods.description: Returns both canvas dimensions as `(width, height)`.
methods.full_doc:
Returns both canvas dimensions as `(width, height)`.

# Returns
`integer, integer` — Width and height in pixels.
methods.returns_doc: `integer, integer` — Width and height in pixels.

methods.name: isCapturing
methods.description: Returns `true` while the stack is between `beginCapture` and `endCapture`.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` while the stack is between `beginCapture` and `endCapture`.

Useful for guard checks in Lua to avoid nested capture calls.
# Returns
`boolean`.

quest.description:
Lua API bindings for `luna.quest.*`.

Exposes quest creation, stage management, objective tracking, and journal
entries through the `luna.quest` table.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
details for quest api-related operations and data management.
Key types exported from this module: `LuaObjective`, `LuaQuestStage`, `LuaQuest`, `LuaQuestLog`.
Primary functions: `register()`.

All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context
and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

details for savegame api-related operations and data management.

All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context
and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

scene.description:
Registers the `luna.scene.*` scene stack, registry, data store, and depth-sorter API.

The scene stack manages a LIFO stack of Lua scene tables with optional
visual transitions. Scene tables are stored via `mlua::RegistryKey` and
identified by `SceneId`. Inter-scene data is stored similarly.

The `DepthSorter` UserData batches draw callbacks by depth for ordered
compositing each frame.

stats.description:
Lua API bindings for the `luna.stats` RPG character sheet system.

Exposes [`Sheet`] and a global [`StatsRegistry`] as `luna.stats.*` Lua values.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
details for stats api-related operations and data management.
Key types exported from this module: `LuaSheet`.
Primary functions: `register()`.

All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context
and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

thread.description:
Registers the `luna.thread` namespace.

Provides Lua-level multithreading: create background threads that run
independent Lua VMs and communicate via thread-safe channels.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
details for thread api-related operations and data management.
Key types exported from this module: `LuaThreadHandle`.
Primary functions: `register()`.

All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context
and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

functions.name: newThread
functions.description: Create a new background thread from a Lua code string.

functions.name: newChannel
functions.description: Create an unnamed thread-safe channel for inter-thread communication.
functions.full_doc:
Create an unnamed thread-safe channel for inter-thread communication.
@return any

functions.name: getChannel
functions.description: Get or create a named global channel (singleton, shared across threads).

ThreadHandle.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a background thread handle.

methods.name: start
methods.description: Launches the background thread, passing optional arguments to the Lua script via `...`.
methods.full_doc:
Launches the background thread, passing optional arguments to the Lua script via `...`.
@param args : MultiValue

# Parameters
- `...` — `any`: Optional arguments forwarded to the thread script as Lua values.
methods.params_doc: - `...` — `any`: Optional arguments forwarded to the thread script as Lua values.

methods.name: wait
methods.description: Blocks the calling coroutine until this thread finishes execution.
methods.full_doc: Blocks the calling coroutine until this thread finishes execution.

methods.name: isRunning
methods.description: Returns `true` if the thread is currently executing.
methods.full_doc:
Returns `true` if the thread is currently executing.
@return any

# Returns
`boolean`.

methods.name: getError
methods.description: Returns the error message if the thread terminated with a Lua error, or `nil` if it completed normally.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the error message if the thread terminated with a Lua error, or `nil` if it completed normally.
@return any

# Returns
`string` — error message, or `nil`.

timer.description:
Timer Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
details for timer api-related operations and data management.
Primary functions: `register()`.

All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context
and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

functions.name: getDelta
functions.description: Returns the delta time (seconds) for the current frame.
functions.full_doc:
Returns the delta time (seconds) for the current frame.
@return any

functions.name: getFPS
functions.description: Returns the measured frames-per-second for the current frame.
functions.full_doc:
Returns the measured frames-per-second for the current frame.
@return any

functions.name: getTime
functions.description: Returns the total elapsed time in seconds since engine start.
functions.full_doc:
Returns the total elapsed time in seconds since engine start.
@return any

functions.name: getAverageDelta
functions.description: Returns the rolling-average frame delta time in seconds.
functions.full_doc:
Returns the rolling-average frame delta time in seconds.
@return any

functions.name: step
functions.description: Advances the timer by one step (called automatically each frame).
functions.full_doc:
Advances the timer by one step (called automatically each frame).
@return any

functions.name: getMicroTime
functions.description: Returns the high-resolution monotonic timer value in microseconds.
functions.full_doc:
Returns the high-resolution monotonic timer value in microseconds.

# Returns
Elapsed microseconds as a 64-bit integer.
functions.returns_doc: Elapsed microseconds as a 64-bit integer.

functions.name: sleep
functions.description: Suspends execution for the given number of seconds.
functions.full_doc:
Suspends execution for the given number of seconds.
@param seconds : number

functions.name: newScheduler
functions.description: Creates an independent timer scheduler for managing a set of callbacks.
functions.full_doc:
Creates an independent timer scheduler for managing a set of callbacks.

# Returns
New Scheduler object with its own after/every/update interface.
functions.returns_doc: New Scheduler object with its own after/every/update interface.

methods.name: after
methods.description: Calls a Lua function after the given delay in seconds.

methods.name: cancel
cancelNamed
methods.description: Cancels a specific named timer that was scheduled on this scheduler.
methods.params_doc: - `name` — Name string used when the timer was created.

methods.name: cancelAll
methods.description: Cancels and removes every pending timer entry from this scheduler.
methods.full_doc:
Cancels and removes every pending timer entry from this scheduler.
@return any

methods.name: pause
methods.description: Pauses the scheduler so no pending callbacks fire until resumed.

methods.name: resume
methods.description: Resumes a paused scheduler, allowing its callbacks to fire again.

methods.name: isPaused
methods.description: Returns whether the scheduler is currently paused and not advancing timers.
methods.returns_doc: true if paused, false if running.

methods.name: getRemaining
methods.description: Returns the time remaining before the next invocation of the given timer.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the time remaining before the next invocation of the given timer.
@param id : integer
@return any

# Parameters
- `id` — Timer ID returned when the timer was created.
# Returns
Remaining time in seconds.
methods.params_doc: - `id` — Timer ID returned when the timer was created.

methods.name: getInterval
methods.description: Returns the repeat interval of the given timer entry in seconds.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the repeat interval of the given timer entry in seconds.
@param id : integer
@return any

# Parameters
- `id` — Timer ID returned when the timer was created.
# Returns
Interval in seconds, or nil for one-shot timers.
methods.params_doc: - `id` — Timer ID returned when the timer was created.
methods.returns_doc: Interval in seconds, or nil for one-shot timers.

methods.name: getRepeatCount
methods.description: Returns how many times the given timer has fired since it was created.
methods.full_doc:
Returns how many times the given timer has fired since it was created.
@param id : integer
@return any

# Parameters
- `id` — Timer ID.
# Returns
Fire count as an integer.

methods.name: getCount
methods.description: Returns the number of active timer entries currently in the scheduler.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the number of active timer entries currently in the scheduler.
@return integer

# Returns
Active timer count as an integer.
methods.returns_doc: Active timer count as an integer.

methods.name: isEmpty
methods.description: Returns whether the scheduler has no active timer entries.
methods.full_doc:
Returns whether the scheduler has no active timer entries.
@return boolean

# Returns
true if the scheduler queue is empty.
methods.returns_doc: true if the scheduler queue is empty.

methods.name: setInterval
methods.description: Changes the repeat interval of an existing timer without recreating it.
methods.params_doc:
- `id` — Timer ID.
- `interval` — New interval in seconds.

methods.name: resetEvent
methods.description: Resets the elapsed time of the given timer so it fires again after its full interval.

methods.name: setTimeScale
methods.description: Sets a time-scale factor that speeds up or slows down all timers in the scheduler.
methods.full_doc:
Sets a time-scale factor that speeds up or slows down all timers in the scheduler.
@param scale : number

# Parameters
- `scale` — Time multiplier (1.0 = real time, 2.0 = double speed).
methods.params_doc: - `scale` — Time multiplier (1.0 = real time, 2.0 = double speed).

methods.description: Returns the current time-scale factor applied to all timers in this scheduler.
methods.full_doc:
Returns the current time-scale factor applied to all timers in this scheduler.
@return any

# Returns
Time scale as a number (1.0 = real time, 0.5 = half speed).
methods.returns_doc: Time scale as a number (1.0 = real time, 0.5 = half speed).

methods.name: update
methods.description: Advances all pending timers by dt seconds.

ai::agent.module_doc: Autonomous agent with kinematic state and attached decision subsystems.  An [`Agent`] is the fundamental unit of AI behavior in Luna2D. Each agent carries its own position, velocity, kinematic constraints (max speed and force), and a [`DecisionModel`] that determines which AI subsystems are ticked during the world's `update(dt)` pass.  Agents are always owned by an [`AIWorld`](crate::ai::world::AIWorld) and are created via `AIWorld::add_agent()`. They are never instantiated standalone in production — the world manages their lifecycle, priority ordering, and blackboard parent-chain wiring.  ## Decision Models  The [`DecisionModel`] enum selects which combination of subsystems the world ticks for this agent each frame:  - `Fsm` — only the [`StateMachine`](crate::ai::fsm::StateMachine) - `Bt` — only the [`BehaviorTree`](crate::ai::behavior_tree::BehaviorTree) - `Steering` — only the [`SteeringManager`](crate::ai::steering::SteeringManager) - `FsmSteering` — FSM first, then steering - `BtSteering` — BT first, then steering  ## Blackboard Hierarchy  Each agent has a local [`Blackboard`]. When created via `AIWorld::add_agent()`, the local blackboard's parent is set to the world's global blackboard, forming a two-level lookup chain: local → global. Writes always go to the local store; reads walk the chain until a match is found.

items.name: DecisionModel
items.description: Controls which AI subsystems are ticked for an agent during `AIWorld::update`.  Each variant maps to a specific combination of decision-making subsystems. The world checks this field to decide whether to tick the agent's FSM, BehaviorTree, SteeringManager, or a combination thereof. The order matters: when both FSM/BT and steering are active, the decision layer runs first (potentially setting steering targets), then steering computes forces.  Lua scripts set this via `agent:setDecisionModel("fsm+steering")` etc.  # Variants - `Fsm` — Fsm variant. - `Bt` — Bt variant. - `Steering` — Steering variant. - `FsmSteering` — FsmSteering variant. - `BtSteering` — BtSteering variant.

items.name: Agent
items.description: An autonomous AI agent with kinematic state and pluggable decision subsystems.  Each agent lives inside an [`AIWorld`](crate::ai::world::AIWorld) and carries:  - **Kinematic state**: position, velocity, max_speed, max_force — used by   steering behaviors to compute and clamp movement forces. - **Decision model**: selects which subsystems (FSM, BT, steering) are ticked. - **Subsystem indices**: optional indices into the world's FSM/BT/steering   storage arrays, linking this agent to its attached decision-makers. - **Blackboard**: local key-value store with a parent chain to the world's   global blackboard for hierarchical data lookup. - **Tags**: string-based labels for group queries and filtering (e.g.,   `"enemy"`, `"flying"`, `"boss"`).  Agents are ticked in descending `priority` order by the world's update loop. Higher priority agents run first, allowing leaders to update blackboard state before followers read it.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `priority` — `i32`. - `position` — `(f32, f32)`. - `velocity` — `(f32, f32)`. - `max_speed` — `f32`. - `max_force` — `f32`. - `decision_model` — `DecisionModel`. - `blackboard` — `Blackboard`. - `tags` — `HashSet<String>`. - `fsm_index` — `Option<usize>`. - `bt_index` — `Option<usize>`. - `steering_index` — `Option<usize>`.

ai::behavior_tree.module_doc: Behavior Tree with composite, decorator, and leaf nodes.  A behavior tree is a hierarchical decision-making structure where each node returns a [`BTStatus`] (Success, Failure, or Running) after being ticked. The tree is traversed from root each frame. Composite nodes (Selector, Sequence, Parallel) manage child execution order. Decorator nodes (Inverter, Repeater, Succeeder) modify child results. Leaf nodes (Action, Condition) call Lua callbacks to evaluate game state or perform actions.  ## Running State and Multi-Frame Execution  When a leaf node returns `Running`, composite nodes memo the running-child index so the next tick resumes from that child instead of restarting from the first child. This enables multi-frame behaviors like "walk to target" that take several frames to complete.  ## Lua Integration  Leaf callbacks are stored as `mlua::RegistryKey` references: - **Action**: `fn(agent_table, blackboard_table, dt) → "success" | "failure" | "running"` - **Condition**: `fn(agent_table, blackboard_table) → bool`  The tree itself does not call these callbacks — that happens at the [`AIWorld`](crate::ai::world::AIWorld) level during `update(dt)`.

items.name: BTStatus
items.description: Execution status returned by every behavior tree node after a tick.  The three-valued return type is the foundation of BT logic: composites and decorators use it to decide whether to continue, abort, or resume child traversal on the next frame.  # Variants - `Success` — Success variant. - `Failure` — Failure variant. - `Running` — Running variant.

items.name: ParallelPolicy
items.description: Policy for determining when a Parallel composite node succeeds or fails.  Parallel nodes tick all children every frame (unlike Selector/Sequence which stop on the first decisive result). The success and failure policies control how individual child results are aggregated into an overall result.  # Variants - `RequireOne` — RequireOne variant. - `RequireAll` — RequireAll variant.

items.name: BTNode
items.description: A node in the behavior tree. Nodes are organized into three categories:  **Composites** (have multiple children): - `Selector` — tries children left-to-right; returns `Success` on the first   child success, `Failure` if all children fail. - `Sequence` — runs children left-to-right; returns `Failure` on the first   child failure, `Success` if all children succeed. - `Parallel` — ticks all children every frame; result depends on the   `success_policy` and `failure_policy`.  **Decorators** (have exactly one child): - `Inverter` — flips Success ↔ Failure, passes Running through unchanged. - `Repeater` — repeats the child N times (0 = infinite loop). - `Succeeder` — always returns Success regardless of child result.  **Leaves** (no children, call Lua callbacks): - `Action` — calls `fn(agent, bb, dt) → "success"|"failure"|"running"`. - `Condition` — calls `fn(agent, bb) → bool` (true → Success, false → Failure).  Composite nodes store a `running_idx` that memos which child was Running last frame, allowing the tree to resume mid-sequence without restarting from the first child.  # Variants - `Selector` — Selector variant. - `Sequence` — Sequence variant. - `Parallel` — Parallel variant. - `Inverter` — Inverter variant. - `Repeater` — Repeater variant. - `Succeeder` — Succeeder variant. - `Action` — Action variant. - `Condition` — Condition variant.

items.name: BehaviorTree
items.description: Root container for a behavior tree instance.  Wraps an optional root [`BTNode`] and caches the [`BTStatus`] from the last tick. The `last_status` is useful for external code (e.g., the Lua API) to query what the tree decided without re-ticking it.  An empty tree (root = None) is valid — it returns `Success` without doing anything.  # Fields - `root` — `Option<BTNode>`. - `last_status` — `BTStatus`.

ai::blackboard.module_doc: Typed key-value store with optional parent chain for hierarchical lookup.  Blackboards are the primary mechanism for sharing named data between AI subsystems in Luna2D. Each [`Agent`](crate::ai::agent::Agent) has a local blackboard; each [`Squad`](crate::ai::squad::Squad) has a squad-level blackboard; and the [`AIWorld`](crate::ai::world::AIWorld) has a global one.  ## Hierarchical Lookup  Blackboards can be chained: an agent's blackboard has the world's global blackboard as its parent. When reading a key, the lookup walks the parent chain until a match is found or the chain ends. Writes always target the local store — they never propagate to parents.  ## Supported Value Types  Three value types are supported via [`BlackboardValue`]: - `Number(f64)` — numeric values - `Bool(bool)` — boolean flags - `Text(String)` — string data  Each type has dedicated get/set methods that enforce type safety at the Rust level. The Lua API layer provides a unified `bb:get(key)` interface.

items.name: BlackboardValue
items.description: A typed value stored in a blackboard slot.  Three types are supported, matching the primitive types commonly passed between Lua callbacks and the AI subsystem. Complex data structures should be decomposed into multiple blackboard entries rather than stored as a single serialized blob.  # Variants - `Number` — Number variant. - `Bool` — Bool variant. - `Text` — Text variant.

items.name: Blackboard
items.description: A hierarchical key-value store for sharing named data between AI subsystems.  Used by agents, squads, and the AI world to communicate without direct coupling. For example, an FSM state callback can write `"target_x"` to the agent's blackboard, and a steering behavior can read it to compute forces.  ## Lookup Rules  - `get_*` methods first check local entries, then walk the parent chain. - `set_*` methods always write to the local store only. - `has()` checks both local and parent stores. - `remove()` and `clear()` affect only the local store.  ## Cloning  Blackboards implement `Clone`. Cloning creates a deep copy including the parent chain. This is used when snapshotting state but should be avoided in per-frame code due to allocation cost.  # Fields - `entries` — `HashMap<String, BlackboardValue>`. - `parent` — `Option<Box<Blackboard>>`.

ai::command_queue.module_doc: RTS-style ordered command queue for scheduling unit actions.  A [`CommandQueue`] manages a FIFO sequence of [`Command`] entries for a single unit or agent. Commands are dequeued from the front and ticked each frame via their Lua callback. Three insertion modes are supported:  - **`enqueue`** — appends a command to the back (standard queue behavior). - **`push_front`** — inserts at the front, interrupting the current command   without clearing the rest of the queue. - **`replace`** — clears the entire queue and enqueues one new command.  Commands carry metadata: a `kind` string (e.g., `"move"`, `"attack"`, `"patrol"`), target coordinates, a priority hint, and an `interruptible` flag that controls whether `cancel_current()` can remove it.  ## Lua Callback Contract  Each command's `callback` is a Lua function `fn(dt) → bool`: - Returns `true` if the command is still running (tick again next frame). - Returns `false` if the command is done (advance to next in queue).  The AIWorld or Lua game loop is responsible for ticking the front command each frame and calling `advance()` when the callback returns `false`.

items.name: Command
items.description: A single RTS unit command with metadata and a Lua tick callback.  Commands are stored in a [`CommandQueue`] and processed one at a time, front-to-back. Each frame the front command's callback is ticked with the frame's delta time. When the callback signals completion, the command is removed and the next one becomes active.  # Fields - `kind` — `String`. - `callback` — `RegistryKey`. - `target_x` — `f32`. - `target_y` — `f32`. - `priority` — `i32`. - `interruptible` — `bool`.

items.name: CommandQueue
items.description: A FIFO queue of [`Command`] entries for sequential unit action scheduling.  Commands are consumed from the front. The game loop ticks the front command each frame and calls `advance()` to move to the next when it completes. Three insertion patterns are supported:  - `enqueue()` — adds to the back (normal queue append). - `push_front()` — inserts at front, pre-empting the current command. - `replace()` — clears everything and enqueues a single new command.  `cancel_current()` removes the front command only if its `interruptible` flag is set, providing a way for players to cancel commands while protecting non-interruptible actions (like scripted animations).  # Fields - `commands` — `VecDeque<Command>`.

ai::flowfield.module_doc: Dijkstra-based flow field for efficient crowd pathfinding.  This is a thin re-export of the canonical implementation in [`crate::pathfinding::ai_flow_field`]. Re-exported here so that the `luna.ai.*` Lua API can access flow fields through a unified AI namespace without requiring users to know the underlying module layout.  See the `pathfinding` module for the full implementation details.

ai::fsm.module_doc: Finite State Machine with priority-ordered guarded transitions.  The FSM is a classic game AI pattern: the agent is in exactly one named state at a time. Each state has optional Lua lifecycle callbacks (`on_enter`, `on_update`, `on_exit`). Transitions between states are guarded by optional Lua predicates and ordered by descending priority.  ## Update Cycle  During each `AIWorld::update(dt)` tick for an agent with `DecisionModel::Fsm`:  1. All transitions from the current state are checked in descending priority    order. The first transition whose guard returns `true` (or has no guard)    fires. 2. If a transition fires: the current state's `on_exit` is called, the    current state switches, `time_in_state` resets to 0, and the new state's    `on_enter` is called. 3. The current state's `on_update(dt)` is called.  ## Priority Ordering  Transitions are stored sorted by descending priority. When multiple transitions from the same source state could fire, the highest-priority one wins. This allows "panic" transitions (e.g., health < 10% → flee) to override normal patrol logic without complex guard nesting.

items.name: StateCallbacks
